name: Pork Chops with Corn Dressing
recipeIngredient: 17 ounces Whole kernel corn, drained or
10-3/4 ounces Condensed cream of mushroom soup, undiluted
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine egg, bread crumbs, corn, water, green pepper, onion, and Worcestershire sauce; set aside.
2. In a large oven-proof skillet or Dutch oven, heat oil over medium-high.
3. Lightly brown pork chops on both sides. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Top each pork chop with the corn dressing mixture.
5. Add enough water to cover the bottom of the pan.
6. Bake at 350°F for about 1 hour, or until the pork is tender. Add additional water to the pan if necessary.
7. Remove pork chops and dressing to a serving platter; keep warm.
8. Add soup and milk to the pan drippings. Cook and stir over medium heat until hot and bubbly.
9. Serve with pork chops. Yield: 6 servings.
